title: Gigantes Plaki
subtitle: Baked Greek butter beans in tomato and herbs
excerpt: Gigantes plaki is a Greek baked dish of large white beans in a thick tomato sauce with garlic, herbs, and olive oil. It is deeply flavoured from the long oven bake and substantial enough to eat as a main course with bread. It keeps well and improves overnight.
sourceNotes: Gigantes plaki, meaning baked giants, refers to the large white beans (gigantes beans) traditional in this dish. Butter beans are the closest available substitute in the UK. A staple of Greek home cooking and a common meze dish, often eaten at room temperature.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely and freeze in portions for up to 3 months. Thaw and reheat gently on the hob, adding a splash of water if needed.
recipe.batchNotes: Makes well in large quantities. Good for meal-prep; keeps in the fridge for 5 days.

content.text: Gigantes plaki is a Greek bake of large white beans in a thick, herb-rich tomato sauce. The beans absorb the sauce as they bake and become creamy at the centre. It is one of the great simple dishes in Greek cooking: inexpensive, filling, and good warm or at room temperature.

Heat the oven to 180°C. Warm {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Cook {{onion}} finely chopped onions for 10 minutes until soft and starting to colour. Add {{garlic}} of sliced garlic and cook for 2 minutes more.
Add the tinned tomatoes, {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée, dried oregano, bay leaves, and cinnamon if using. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Tip the drained butter beans into a large baking dish. Pour the tomato sauce over the top and stir to combine. The beans should be mostly submerged. Add a splash of water if they look dry.

Bake uncovered for 50 to 60 minutes, stirring once halfway through, until the sauce is thick and the beans on top are slightly caramelised at the edges. Scatter with parsley and drizzle with more olive oil before serving.

items.symptom: The beans are drying out in the oven
items.cause: Not enough sauce, or oven too hot
items.fix: Add a splash of water and stir. The beans absorb a lot of liquid during baking; check and add water as needed if the dish looks dry before the cooking time is up.

items.symptom: The sauce is thin after baking
items.cause: Tinned tomatoes too watery, or not enough time in the oven
items.fix: Continue baking uncovered for another 15 minutes. The sauce concentrates as it reduces in the oven.

items.symptom: The beans are firm rather than creamy
items.cause: Tinned beans can vary in texture
items.fix: The baking should soften them further. If they're still firm after the full time, cover the dish with foil and bake for another 20 minutes.

content.text: Gigantes plaki is eaten across Greece as a meze dish, a side dish, and a light main course. It is particularly associated with fasting periods in Orthodox religious practice, when it provides a filling, protein-rich meal without meat. At room temperature with bread it makes a good lunch; warm as part of a bigger spread it holds its own alongside grilled meats.
